typography.headings: Playfair Display, Cormorant Garamond, or Cinzel (fantasy/mystical vibe)
typography.body: Inter or similar clean font, but headings must be magical/serif

weights.dary_message: font-light italic text-yellow-300 text-lg md:text-xl mt-2

colors.background: bg-neutral-950 with a radial glow in the center from #1a1a1a to #0a0a0a

visual_enhancers.background_texture: Add a subtle noise overlay and slow-moving floating particles in the background using framer-motion to make the page feel alive, magical, and fitting for The Neverending Story.
visual_enhancers.auryn: Hover scaling, infinite slow rotation or pulsing glow when idle. Click triggers a bright flash or accelerated spin.
visual_enhancers.names_idle: When floating, names should wander around smoothly. Apply a text-shadow so they are always readable.
visual_enhancers.layout_animations: Use framer-motion's layoutId for smooth, spectacular transitions when names move from floating random positions to their corner groups.

layouts.container: relative w-full h-screen overflow-hidden bg-neutral-950 text-white font-sans selection:bg-yellow-500/30
layouts.corners: absolute w-auto max-w-[300px] p-8 flex flex-col gap-4 z-20
layouts.center: absolute inset-0 m-auto w-[300px] h-[300px] z-50 flex flex-col items-center justify-center gap-6

motion.floating_state: Map over all names. For each name, calculate an initial random fixed top (10% to 90%) and left (10% to 90%) on mount. Render them absolute in the floating_container. To make them wander, use framer-motion animate with an array of random x and y values, transition duration ~20-30s, ease linear, repeat Infinity, repeatType mirror.
motion.sorted_state: Render the 4 corner containers and map their respective names inside them as standard flex children. Render Dary in the center container under the Auryn. Because all names use layoutId={name}, Framer Motion will magically fly them from their random wandering absolute positions to their structured flex-box grid in the corners.
motion.dary_message: Render conditionally when sorted state is true. Use initial={{opacity: 0, y: 10}} animate={{opacity: 1, y: 0}} transition={{delay: 1.5, duration: 1}} so it elegantly fades in AFTER the names finish flying.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure floating names have text-shadow (e.g. drop-shadow-md or text-shadow) to stand out against the dark magical background.
accessibility.auryn_button: Auryn image must be wrapped in a semantic button or have role='button' and tabIndex={0} with onKeyDown support. Add data-testid='auryn-trigger'.

instructions_to_main_agent: Build a single-page React app that implements the Escape Room sorting logic.
DO NOT USE standard generic UI or generic Vercel layouts. This must be a magical, spectacular visual experience matching The Neverending Story.
Use framer-motion heavily. `layoutId` is MANDATORY for the sorting effect to look like magic.
Define a React state `const [isSorted, setIsSorted] = useState(false)`.

When `!isSorted`, render all 20 names inside the `floating_container`. Give them initial random `left` and `top` percentages (use useMemo or useEffect to set them once so they don't jump on re-render). Wrap them in `<motion.div layoutId={name}>` and apply a wandering animation.
When `isSorted` is true, render the 4 corner group components, each containing a list of their specific members, wrapped in `<motion.div layoutId={name}>`.
This component unmounting from the absolute container and mounting in the corners with identical `layoutId` will trigger Framer Motion's FLIP animation, creating a smooth flying effect.
Dary should have his own `layoutId='Dary'` and fly to the center under the Auryn when sorted.
The 'Ayudante de Jose' text should fade in smoothly after a delay of ~1.5s, once sorting is visually complete.
Corner group titles (Los Comerrocas, etc.) should use the exact specified colors and have a slight mystical glow (drop-shadow).
Ensure the Auryn image pulses or rotates slowly when idle, and has a magical hover effect (scale up + intense glow). When clicked, it should trigger `setIsSorted(true)`.
Add a magical particles or noise overlay for the background to enhance the fantasy aesthetic.
All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute (e.g., data-testid='student-name', data-testid='group-title', data-testid='auryn-trigger').
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic outputs. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Use dark colors independently without gradients for backgrounds if needed, but a central radial glow is fine.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions.
All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute.
Components MUST use named exports, Pages MUST use default exports.
